comments.body:
Added WIP because there are some inefficiencies that could be fixed.
1: I had to copy functions from cmd/podman/shared to find the pid namespace information for the containers. This was the best way to filter duplicates (containers in the same namespace would return the same output twice). I don't know of a solution to this problem.
2: The psgo library doesn't have an option to run without a header, so I had to filter it out manually. @vrothberg is this something you'd be willing to add? If so, I can take a stab at it, but i'm sure you'd handle it much faster if you have time.
Everything else I feel pretty good about.
psgo should definitely take a flag to show no header.
Cool, we'll ping you in the PR for containers/psgo to have a look and provide feedback.
@vrothberg's update fixed both of my issues. Should be good to go. PTAL

@haircommander awesome! There is just one issue with cross compilation:
```
TARGET="darwin.amd64"; \
	GOOS="${TARGET%%.*}" \
	GOARCH="${TARGET##*.}" \
	go build -i -ldflags ' -X main.gitCommit="9db0435d7da50e35f925e77055611712983bc4f8" -X main.buildInfo=1534973108' -tags 'containers_image_openpgp containers_image_ostree_stub exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper exclude_graphdriver_overlay' -o "bin/podman.cross.darwin.amd64" github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman
# github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman
cmd/podman/pod_top.go:90:22: pod.GetPodPidInformation undefined (type *libpod.Pod has no field or method GetPodPidInformation)
```
We need something like `libpod/pod_top_unsupported.go` to export the stubs to make the builds happy.

It might seem like bikeshedding, but I really value expressive commit messages. They're invaluable when doing research and help a lot when debugging etc. Can you add some descriptions to the commits?

Despite these little nitpicks, code LGTM.

:pushpin: Commit 1d8b5ca has been approved by `mheon`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mheon 1d8b5cad5e559b5682d2215ad8ab6e2b275f8219 -->

Approved by: mheon
